human_verification.notes: Reviewed boundary enforcement, authority source discipline, disagreement handling, disclosure-risk gating, and scenario coverage against validated thresholds.

specialist_owned_evidence: Specialty output is bound to elder care and disability records (MDS/OASIS/HCBS/ICF context) rather than generic analytics.
Authority sources include CMS quality frameworks, CFR requirements, HIPAA disclosure controls, and recognized disability outcomes references.
Evaluation scenarios cover conflicts, data gaps, ambiguity, unsupported causal claims, disclosure risk, and adjacent-lane boundaries.
Functionality map explicitly links all scenarios to required abilities and marks scenario coverage as complete.

review_notes: Boundary safety remains explicit around clinical, legal, and payer adjudication requests.

notes: The pack is designed as a validated spec package with explicit refusal and escalation behavior for safety, privacy, and authority boundaries.
No cross-setting, non-elder/disability defaulting is performed without tenant context and explicit approval.
